taskDescription: BCOS Dashboard cockpit-and-drawer rebuild, Steps 4-7. Move the four standalone panels still on the main page into either a click-driven side drawer (jobs panel) or a /settings route (run history, file health, snapshot canary). Then polish empty/loading/error states and accessibility/responsive/dark-mode. Each step ships independently as its own commit.

previousCommits.title: Phase 4: filter chips for run history
Step 3: attention inbox promoted into the cockpit

discoveryResults.skillsRelationToPlan: The dashboard READS from artifacts these skills produce (digest, diary, schedule-config) but does not modify any skill in this plan. schedule-tune NL skill remains the canonical free-form path; the dashboard's preset buttons (already shipped) are the deterministic complement.

phases: Phase 1: Drawer pattern (Step 4) — slide-in side drawer for per-job detail; remove standalone jobs_panel from main page
Phase 2: /settings route (Step 5) — real URL with back navigation; move run history, file health, snapshot canary off main page
Phase 3: States (Step 6) — empty / first-run / loading / error / partial / stale-snapshot
Phase 4: A11y + responsive + dark mode (Step 7) — color contrast, screen-reader labels, keyboard nav, mobile layout, prefers-color-scheme dark
Phase 5 (FIXED END): Integration audit + ecosystem state + learnings capture

tasks.content: Add drawer primitives to dashboard.html shell (overlay + aside elements already present from local-dashboard-builder template — verify they exist or add)
tasks.activeForm: Adding drawer primitives to HTML shell

tasks.content: Create new collector collect_job_detail(job_id) in single_repo.py — returns rich per-job payload (description, today's digest body, recent runs, schedule presets, technical-details footer)
Add GET /api/job/<id> endpoint in server.py that calls collect_job_detail with the path parameter
Implement window.JOB_DRAWER controller in dashboard.js: open(job_id), close(), keyboard Esc binding, single-instance guard, focus trap inside drawer when open

Wire cockpit dot click → JOB_DRAWER.open(job_id). Replace the current scroll-to-card flash with a real drawer open. Same for cockpit attention items where source_job is known: clicking opens the originating job's drawer

Drawer body markup: header (name + verdict badge + close button), schedule line, last/next run line, WHAT IT DOES paragraph, TODAY'S RESULT body from digest, RECENT RUNS list (5 verdict dots + label), CHANGE FREQUENCY preset buttons (reuse _renderSchedulePresets), collapsed Technical details footer (raw IDs, ISO timestamps, schedule-config path, fingerprint)

Drawer CSS: 480px wide on desktop, slide-in from right with 250ms ease, overlay at rgba(0,0,0,0.32), border-radius left side, body lock-scroll while open. Respect prefers-reduced-motion (no slide, just appear)

Remove the standalone jobs_panel Panel from run.py panels(). The /api/panel/jobs_panel endpoint stays (the drawer composes its data from /api/job/<id> directly, but jobs_panel cache remains the primary data source for the cockpit dots)

Browser-verify via preview_eval: click each dot opens correct drawer, Esc closes, two clicks on different dots crossfade, click outside closes, mark-done a finding inside drawer triggers cockpit refresh
Commit: feat(dashboard): Step 4 — per-job drawer

Add window.history.pushState routing in dashboard.js: /, /settings, /settings/runs, /settings/files, /settings/schedules, /settings/technical. popstate listener navigates between them. Back button works.

Add a render mode switch in app init: if pathname starts with /settings, render the settings layout instead of the cockpit. Server.py serves the same HTML for any unknown path; routing happens client-side.
Update server.py do_GET so paths under /settings/ all return the same dashboard.html (SPA fallback). Don't 404 unknown paths that aren't /api/* or /static/*.
Build settings shell in dashboard.js: top bar with [← Back] button + repo name, side nav with 4 sections (Run history / File health / Schedules / Technical), main content area swaps based on subpath
Settings: Run history page — wraps existing renderRunHistory output. Filter chips work as today.
Settings: File health page — wraps existing renderFileHealth output. One-click fixes still POST to existing endpoints.

Settings: Schedules page — bulk view of all jobs with their preset buttons in one table. Auto-fix whitelist toggles (additions/removals POST to /api/schedule/whitelist — new endpoint wired to set_auto_fix_whitelist).

Settings: Technical page — snapshot freshness canary (verbose form), MCP refresh hint with copy-to-clipboard, raw schedule-config.json viewer, env-var dump, debug info (BCOS_REPO_ROOT, BCOS_TASK_ID, dashboard version)
Add ⚙ Settings link in cockpit header (top-right). Clicking pushes /settings; pressing Back returns to /. Both directions update the URL.

Remove standalone snapshot_freshness, run_history, file_health Panels from run.py panels(). Their collectors still feed /api/panel/<id> endpoints (used by settings sub-pages) and remain on the cockpit's payload (freshness still surfaces in headline when stale).

Browser-verify: navigate to /settings/runs, /settings/files, /settings/schedules, /settings/technical via clicks; back button returns to cockpit; URL updates; refresh on /settings/foo loads the right sub-page.
Commit: feat(dashboard): Step 5 — /settings route + remove standalone detail panels from main page

Empty state: when action_count=0 AND all dots green AND no stale snapshot — collapse the cockpit to a tight one-line confirmation: 'Your knowledge system is healthy. Nothing needs your attention.' Maintenance strip stays as a quiet 5 ● dots row.

First-run state: detected when diary file is empty/missing AND no schedule-config exists. Cockpit shows onboarding copy: 'Welcome to BCOS. Your first maintenance check runs tomorrow at 9 AM. Come back to see what it finds.' with a link to docs/_bcos-framework/ for setup.

Loading state: replace 'Loading panels…' fallback with skeleton blocks matching the cockpit shape (eyebrow strip + headline placeholder + 3-row attention placeholder + 5-dot placeholder). Skeletons fade out as data arrives.

API error per-section: when a collector returns {error: ...}, render a small error band ('We couldn't load this section. [Retry]') instead of the raw trace. Tiny 'details' disclosure shows trace for power users. Other sections keep working.

Stale snapshot in cockpit: verify the headline-level warning ('Heads up — background data is X old. Refresh when you can.') reads naturally and the cockpit border-tone is appropriate (warn, not critical for >24h; critical for missing).

Partial errors: if 1 of 5 jobs errored in collect_jobs_panel, the cockpit still renders the other 4 dots normally and shows the 5th as a small ⚠ glyph with hover='collect failed: <reason>'. The whole cockpit doesn't go critical for one job's collect error.
Browser-verify each state by mocking conditions (empty actions-resolved, missing diary, slow collector, broken collector). Take screenshots / preview_snapshots for each.
Commit: feat(dashboard): Step 6 — empty / first-run / loading / error / partial states

Color contrast audit: check every text/background combo against WCAG AA (4.5:1 for body, 3:1 for large). Run a pass with axe-core or manual eye + browser inspector. Fix offenders by darkening text or lightening background.

Screen-reader labels: every interactive element gets aria-label. Job cards: aria-label='Documentation check, healthy, last run earlier this morning'. Dots: aria-label='Documentation check, healthy'. Mark-done buttons: aria-label='Mark <title> done'. Replace icon-only buttons with aria-labels.

Keyboard navigation: explicit tabindex order: skip-to-content link → headline → attention items (each row tabbable + buttons inside) → dots (left/right arrow nav within strip) → settings link. Test with keyboard only.
Focus indicators: 2px outline in severity color (or info blue for neutral elements). Verify visible on all interactive elements. Test with browser :focus-visible behavior.

Reduced motion: audit all animations. The drawer slide, the mark-done fade, the highlight flash, the cockpit count badge update, the schedule preset save toast — all wrapped in @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) overrides that swap to instant transitions.

Mobile breakpoint <768px: drawer becomes full-screen bottom sheet (slides up not from right). Cockpit dots become horizontal scroll strip. Attention items collapse to single line + tap to expand. Hit targets ≥44×44px (current preset chips at 22px need uppsizing on mobile).

Dark mode: add @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) overrides. Use dark-grey base (not pure black). Re-derive --color-severity-*-bg variants for dark mode (lower alpha works against dark surface). Headline serif italic gets a slight color lift against dark base.

Manual screen-reader test: NVDA or Narrator. Verify: cockpit reads as a coherent paragraph. Job cards read with verdict + last-run inline. Mark-done buttons announce action + target. Dots are reachable.
Commit: feat(dashboard): Step 7 — accessibility, responsive, dark mode

Run python .claude/scripts/analyze_integration.py --staged from business-context-os-dev to catch any stale cross-references in skill docs that might mention removed panels (jobs_panel, run_history, file_health, snapshot_freshness as standalone)

Update docs/_bcos-framework references to mention the new IA: cockpit + drawer + /settings route. Specifically check docs that might reference 'jobs panel' or panel section numbers (§01 etc) — those are gone.
Update bcos-dashboard/README.md to reflect the cockpit-first IA. New screenshots / mockup references. Note that /settings is the home for power-user views.

Capture learnings via ecosystem-manager skill: what worked (translation map → dramatic UX win in one commit; clear-planner manifest enabling fresh-session resumption), surprises (preview_screenshot timeouts; cache-invalidation across composed panels), guidance for future framework UX work.
Final commit: docs(dashboard): update README + framework references for cockpit IA + capture learnings

openQuestions: Drawer for attention items: should clicking an attention item with known source_job open that job's drawer (current proposal in P1_005) or open a separate 'finding detail' drawer? Recommendation: source-job drawer — keeps drawer count low, jobs ARE the natural unit.

Settings sub-pages: tabbed (single URL with state) or each its own route? Plan currently goes route-per-page — better browser back behavior, but more routing code. Tab-style is simpler; URL would be /settings#runs etc. Open for adjustment.

First-run detection: currently proposes 'no diary AND no schedule-config'. But a brand-new BCOS install will have schedule-config (it ships with the framework via update.py). Better detector: 'diary has zero entries'. Adjust accordingly.
Mobile bottom-sheet drawer height: 80vh? Full screen with close handle at top? Recommendation: 90vh with handle, allows peek at the cockpit behind.
followUps: Step 8 (post-Step-7): introduce the /settings/maintenance-history page that aggregates last N=90 days of diary into a small heatmap calendar — currently data is there but no calendar view exists

Step 9: drawer for FILE-level inspection — clicking a file in the file-health list opens a drawer showing its full frontmatter, stale-detection rationale, and one-click 'open in editor' (only useful in dev installs)
Independent: add a /settings/about page with version, link to BCOS framework docs, license — basic chrome that ships with any standalone tool
